checks.a_analysis: The output document correctly captures the text from the source PDF without any hallucinations or invented sections. Structural headings like 'Minutes Vote' and 'Attendance' are present in the source material. The document title and alt text are acceptable AI additions.

The output contains one URL ('NC NextGen911 Days Since Last Upload Dashboard (arcgis.com)') which is correctly transcribed from page 7 of the source document. No fabricated phone numbers, emails, or other URLs were found.

All pages and major sections from the source PDF are present in the output JSON. While some specific details within sections are missing (evaluated in C5), the sections themselves (Agenda, Minutes, Updates) are represented.

The tables in the output match the source document. The attendance table from pages 2 and 3 is split into two tables in the JSON (elements 14 and 18) but contains all the same individuals. The 'Minutes Vote' and 'Current Deployment Service Types' tables also match the row counts in the source.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The output fails to include significant content from nested lists (sub-bullets) on pages 6, 7, and 8. On page 6, the details regarding Seymour Johnson AFB and Cybersecurity committees are omitted. On page 7, the specific readiness status of four jurisdictions (Cleveland, Lincoln, Onslow, Polk) and the demographic statistics (Total Population and Land Mass) are entirely missing. On page 8, the specific detail about the Tier 2 technician onboarding is omitted. These omissions constitute a loss of more than 25% of the information in those specific update sections.
checks.b_evidence_source: 4 jurisdictions remain to reach 100% i3 readiness (as of 5/31/2022). o Cleveland County and Kings Mountain – not scheduled (361 critical errors/73.34%) – given deadline of June 30. o Lincoln County – not scheduled (324 critical errors/97.48%) – aiming for a 5/31 completion. o Onslow County – Q3 migration (867 critical errors/93.24%) - will be complete by mid-June. o Polk County – Q3 migration (561 critical errors/84.61%). ... Statistics o Total Population served by NG 911: 10,477,959 (99%). o Total land mass covered by NG911: 7,328.7 sq. mi. (96%).
checks.b_evidence_output: • 4 jurisdictions remain to reach 100% i3 readiness (as of 5/31/2022). • Statistics

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The output faithfully captures the wording of the source, including original typos found in the scanned document (e.g., 'Macon Count' on page 6, 'altert' on page 7, and 'June 27t' on page 6). Meaning is preserved throughout the transcribed text.

checks.a_analysis: The output document was reviewed for duplicated content blocks. No instances of repeated paragraphs, sections, or list items were found that were not present in the original source document. The headers ('North Carolina 911 Board', etc.) appear on multiple pages in the source and are represented once per page or normalized in the output, which is acceptable.

The heading hierarchy is generally well-preserved. Major titles use H1, and sections use H2 or H3. There are no skips in the hierarchy (e.g., H1 to H3). While some sections on the final pages use H1 for section titles (e.g., 'NMAC' in Section 8), this is a common normalization and does not break the navigational structure for screen readers.

Tables such as the Agenda table on page 1, the Attendance table on pages 2-3, and the Deployment tables on page 5 are correctly structured with appropriate header and data rows. The split of the Attendance table across pages 2 and 3 into two separate JSON objects is an expected behavior of the pipeline and maintains the internal structural integrity of each segment.

There are significant reading order defects. On Page 6, the reading order is reversed: text elements such as the chart caption (element 40), map link (element 41), and project notes (elements 42-44) appear before the images they describe (elements 45-46), which are physically at the top and middle of the page in the source. Additionally, on Page 7, there are gaps in the reading order sequence (missing elements 48 and 51), which correspond to missing content blocks (URLs).
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 6: Bar Chart followed by '*Hosted Viper #49 is Macon Count'. Page 7: GIS Update followed by 'NC Next Generation 911 GIS Services Webpage' link.
checks.b_evidence_output: Section 6: Elements 40-44 appear before element 46 (chart image). Section 7: elements 48 and 51 are missing from the reading_order sequence.

checks.a_analysis: The output consistently fails to represent nested list structures. On Page 6 (element 44) and Page 7 (element 49), top-level list items are present, but their associated sub-bullets (nested items) are entirely omitted from the output. This results in a significant loss of document structure and information.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 7, GIS Update: bullet for '4 jurisdictions...' followed by sub-bullets for Cleveland, Lincoln, Onslow, and Polk.
checks.b_evidence_output: Element 49, item 1: '4 jurisdictions remain to reach 100% i3 readiness (as of 5/31/2022).' (Nested items omitted).

checks.a_analysis: Two URLs clearly visible in the source document on Page 7 are completely missing from the output. These missing links correspond to the gaps identified in the reading order (elements 48 and 51).
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 7: 'NC Next Generation 911 GIS Services Webpage' and 'NC NextGen911 Days Since Last Upload Dashboard (arcgis.com)'.
checks.b_evidence_output: These text blocks/links are absent from Section 7 of the JSON.

checks.a_analysis: The visual organization of the converted document matches the source PDF. The document structure, including the header information, agenda items, attendance tables, and meeting minutes, follows the same logical flow as the original. Multi-column information is handled correctly within tables or vertical blocks, preserving the document's intent.

All tables from the source document (Agenda, Attendance, Minutes Vote, Next Up Migrations, and Deployment Service Types) are correctly rendered in the HTML. They maintain a clear structure with visible borders and aligned columns, making the data easily readable and understandable for a sighted user.

All meaningful images, including the NC DIT 911 Board logos and the 'Summary of PSAPS Live on ESInet' bar chart, are present in the HTML output. The chart image also includes descriptive alt text that accurately reflects the data presented in the original PDF chart.

There is no visual duplication of body content in the HTML output. Each paragraph, table, and section appears only once in the main body, mirroring the source PDF. Navigation elements like the Table of Contents are correctly implemented and do not count as duplication.
